title: Frontier Queues, Visited Reservation, State Transitions, and Determinism · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds race-safe deterministic frontier state machine from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of race-safe deterministic frontier state machine while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, retryable, terminal, and canceled states with legal transitions.
Reserve admitted URL keys atomically before enqueue so concurrent discoveries cannot duplicate effects.
Use stable priority and tie keys for owner commitments, depth, page class, freshness, origin fairness, and retry readiness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Frontier Transition
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 7087 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove legal frontier transitions, reserve-on-schedule, stable heap order, one owner, and state invariants; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Frontier Transition.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define discovered, rejected, queued, claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, retryable, terminal, and canceled states with legal transitions. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Frontier Transition, keep Frontier Transition connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
